mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in quarterly regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal fails to predict direction during tightening periods specifically.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and forces negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that cause rapid portfolio deleveraging and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered quarters or the signal shows no directional edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle signals improving supply capacity that supports equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered periods during easing cycles or the signal loses predictive power when policy rates are declining.
